title: HMW DNA extraction from diverse plants species for PacBio and Nanopore sequencing
author: Alessia Russo
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.5t7g6rn

steps.title: Incubate Lysis Buffer at 65 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate Lysis Buffer at 65 °C for at least 30 minutes (gently mix the tube every 10 minutes). For convenience, one can aliquote 600 µl Lysis Buffer in a 2 ml tube per sample and incubate in the water bath. Keep the SDS Lysis Buffer at 65°C until the tissue powder is added (the buffer has to be warm to ensure that powder is well dissolved). Work under a fume hood until the end of the protocol because of the presence of BME, Phenol and Chloroform.

steps.title: Carefully add ~ 50-100 mg tissue...
steps.instruction: Carefully add ~ 50-100 mg tissue powder (1-2 spoons SP SCIENCEWARE 0.10 ml, see equipment list) to a 2 ml tube with 600 µl of warm SDS Lysis Buffer. Add more than 100 mg of tissue can decrease DNA purity (A260/230). Immediately homogenize by vortexing 3-5 seconds. It’s important to rapidly homogenize the lysate to neutralize DNase, polyphenols,...

steps.title: Incubate 20 min @ 55°C with...
steps.instruction: Incubate 20 min @ 55°C with gentle agitation in a thermomixer (maximum 400 rpm) or in a water bath. Invert the tube gently 10 times after 10 min.

steps.title: Add 4 µl 100mg/ml RNase A,...
steps.instruction: Add 4 µl 100mg/ml RNase A, gently mix by inversion 10 times and incubate 10 minutes at 55 °C with gentle agitation.

steps.title: Add 200 µl 5M KAc
steps.instruction: Add 200 µl 5M KAc. Mix by gently pipetting up and down 5 times with a P1000 cut tip and by inverting the tube 5 times in order to obtain a homogeneous solution to fully precipitate the proteins and the polysaccharides that will complex with SDS.

steps.title: Add 800 µl Phenol:Chloroform:IsyamylAlcohol pH=
steps.instruction: Add 800 µl Phenol:Chloroform:IsyamylAlcohol pH=

steps.title: If you don't have a ready-to-use...
steps.instruction: If you don't have a ready-to-use Phenol:Chloroform:IsyamylAlcohol solution, add 400 µl Phenol pH=8, mix by inverting and add 400 µl Chloroform:IsoamylAlcohol 24:

steps.title: Work under a fume hood
steps.instruction: Work under a fume hood.

steps.title: Incubate 10 min @ 20rpm/min on a rotator
steps.instruction: Incubate 10 min @ 20rpm/min on a rotator.

steps.title: Centrifuge 10 minutes @ 10,000g at RT
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 10 minutes @ 10,000g at RT.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant (usually 700-800 µl)...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant (usually 700-800 µl) in a new 2 ml tube with a P1000 cut tip. Pipette slowly and with a cut tip to avoid breaking the long DNA fragments. Make sure not to carry over the interphase or the lower layer that would contaminate the DNA.

steps.title: Add the same volume of Chloroform:IsoamylAlcohol 24:1
steps.instruction: Add the same volume of Chloroform:IsoamylAlcohol 24:1

steps.title: Incubate at RT for 10 min...
steps.instruction: Incubate at RT for 10 min with gentle agitation (20 rpm)

steps.title: Centrifuge RT 10,000g for 10 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge RT 10,000g for 10 min

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant into a new 2 ml tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant into a new 2 ml tube. Make sure not to carry over the interphase or lower layer that would contaminate the DNA. Determine the final volume.

steps.title: Before starting, vortex the Bead Solution...
steps.instruction: Before starting, vortex the Bead Solution for 30 seconds to ensure beads are completely resuspended. Add 1 volume of Bead Solution to the sample tube (1:1 ratio, v/v).

steps.title: Incubate @10 rpm on a rotator...
steps.instruction: Incubate @10 rpm on a rotator for 20-30 min at RT.

steps.title: Spin down for 1 second
steps.instruction: Spin down for 1 second.

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnetic rack and wait until all beads bind at the back of the tube and the solution becomes clear (this will probably take a few minutes, as the DNA is highly concentrated and HMW).

steps.title: Remove the supernatant without disturbing the bead pellet
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant without disturbing the bead pellet.

steps.title: Add 1 ml EtOH 70% (the washing solution)
steps.instruction: Add 1 ml EtOH 70% (the washing solution).

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and mix by gently inverting the tube 20 times to resuspend the bead pellet in the washing solution (probably it won't be entirely resuspendend and there will be some small/big bead aggregates floating - if that happens, don't worry).

steps.title: Spin down for 1 second
steps.instruction: Spin down for 1 second.

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnetic rack and wait until all beads bind at the back of the tube and the solution becomes clear.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant without disturbing the bead pellet
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant without disturbing the bead pellet.

steps.title: Repeat steps 20 - 24 two-three...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 20 - 24 two-three times (total possible washing = 4 times, but usually 2 washes are enough).

steps.title: Spin down for 1 second and...
steps.instruction: Spin down for 1 second and remove the remaining washing solution with a P10 pipette.

steps.title: Add 50 µl EB (or Tris-HCl 10 mM pH=8
steps.instruction: Add 50 µl EB (or Tris-HCl 10 mM pH=8.5).

steps.title: Resuspend the beads by gently swirling or flicking the tube
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads by gently swirling or flicking the tube. This time they must be completely resuspended. It is important that the beads are not aggregated, or the elution efficiency will be reduced.

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 15...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 15 minutes and flick the tube again.

steps.title: Spin down for 1 second
steps.instruction: Spin down for 1 second.

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnetic rack and wait until all beads bind at the back of the tube and the solution becomes clear. If DNA is at high concentration, this can take a long time. You can leave it overnight at RT, and/or add more EB (if you do so mix by flicking the tube after the addition of EB and put the tube back on the Magnetic Rack).

steps.title: Transfer the eluted DNA into a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the eluted DNA into a new tube, with a cut tip, or using a wide-bore tip, as DNA is very concentrated and the solution very viscous.

steps.title: Leave the DNA at RT overnight,...
steps.instruction: Leave the DNA at RT overnight, as the HMW DNA is very aggregated and needs to get relaxed and homogenized. The day after proceed with Nanodrop lecture, to determine the purity, and Qubit to determine concentration.

steps.title: Grind ~100-110 mg leaf tissue in...
steps.instruction: Grind ~100-110 mg leaf tissue in LN 2 with mortar and pestle. This is a crucial step as it will affect final DNA yield, purity, and integrity. Plant leaves must be flash frozen in LN 2 immediately after harvesting and stored at -80 °C until DNA extraction procedure, if not immediately used. Here a useful tutorial on how to grind plant tissue with LN 2 https://bionanogenomics.com/videos/liquid-nitrogen-grinding-tutorial/ . As they say: "Keep grinding until powder is as fine as wheat flour" Tips to achieve better results: - Apply enough pressure to allow cells to break and recover as much DNA as possible. - Keep the powder frozen until it goes into the lysis buffer , by adding LN2 while grinding (LN2 evaporates quickly). This prevents nuclease activation and oxidation processes (once powder is in the lysis buffer, the nucleases will be inactivated by the SDS and EDTA, and the oxidation blocked by BME and Metabisulfite)

steps.title: An additional purification steps can improve...
steps.instruction: An additional purification steps can improve the A260/A280 and A260/A230 ratios. At this step you can pool the elution of multiple tubes. Before starting: - Incubate the Beads Solution at RT for more than 30 minutes prior to use; - Mix the beads by vortexing for 20 seconds before use . Repeat steps 15 to 33.

steps.title: Determine the purity of the DNA...
steps.instruction: Determine the purity of the DNA at 230, 260, 280 nm with a spectrophotometer (e.g. NanoDrop 2000). Acceptable values are: - A260/280 = 1.8 - 2.0; - A260/230 = 2.0 - 2.2. A260/280 >2.0 or A260/230 >2.2 could be indicative of a contamination by RNA.

steps.title: Determine the concentration of double-stranded DNA...
steps.instruction: Determine the concentration of double-stranded DNA with a fluorimetric-based method (e.g. Qubit 3.0 Fluorometer BR assay). The ratio between the concentration measured with Nanodrop, over the concentration measured by Qubit should be <2.

steps.title: Evaluate DNA integrity on a TapeStation,...
steps.instruction: Evaluate DNA integrity on a TapeStation, and fragment length on PhemtoPulse (or PFGE).
